response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong testing/craftsmanship discipline, but the role centers on modern C++ development for industrial automation, and the resume shows only a brief 6-month C/C++ stint at National Instruments back in 2014-2015. There is no evidence of recent C++/STL work, multi-threaded high-performance systems, message brokers, MQTT/IoT, or IEC 62443/SDL exposure — the bulk of the career is Node.js, TypeScript, and DLang. Skill set is broad and adjacent but not a fit for a Senior Manager C++ role requiring 8-12 years of relevant C++ depth.
strengths: Long tenure (10+ years) as a senior engineer with strong TDD, CI/CD, and software-craftsmanship habits
Distributed/event-driven systems experience (Sociomantic micro-services, GISCollective platform) transferable to message-broker architectures
Mentorship and technical communication proven through workshops, talks, and course creation
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C/C++ experience (2014-2015) and no recent C++/STL work
No demonstrated exposure to IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, MQTT, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
No IoT or industrial automation domain background; career is web/SaaS-oriented

key_factors.factor: Missing industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443/SDL) and IoT protocol exposure
Strong engineering craftsmanship and distributed-systems background
